justification: The brief systematically distinguishes high-confidence areas (API endpoint, format, date range, async pattern) from low-confidence areas (usage metrics, report content, authorization model). High-confidence sections cover GET /reports, csv|pdf format, and date range parameters. Low-confidence sections explicitly flag report content/metrics as undefined — 'usage' is called out as completely undefined by Bird multiple times. The open_questions section includes BLOCKING items that serve as a partial escalation to Bird for missing acceptance criteria. Shaq is given a clear actionable starting point (API scaffolding, input validation, format rendering, async pattern) while being told to isolate metric aggregation behind an interface — this is a partial block, not a full block. No report content is invented; every MJ-originated detail is explicitly labeled as an assumption. Pippen's async generation recommendation is included as an explicit architectural decision with implementation guidance.

observations.text: Exemplary confidence calibration — every architecture decision is attributed to its source (MJ-originated, Pippen-originated, Bird-undefined) with explicit labels
No invented metrics — 'usage' is flagged as undefined throughout, with specific callouts that columns, aggregations, and report schema are unknown
Shaq gets a clear actionable scope: API endpoint structure, format parameter handling, date range filtering, input validation, async pattern — all implementable without Bird's input
Pippen's async generation is promoted to a full architectural decision with a concrete implementation recommendation (sync ≤31 days, async with job ID for longer ranges)
Terminology alignment section explicitly deconstructs Bird's vague terms ('usage', 'visibility', 'time period') and maps them to technical concepts while flagging ambiguity
Open questions are prioritized with BLOCKING labels, effectively functioning as a partial escalation even though the escalations array is empty
The escalations array is empty — the blocking open questions should have been surfaced as a formal escalation to Bird rather than buried in open_questions
Acceptance criteria include 'usage data in the events table' which subtly assumes MJ's data source is correct, though this is flagged elsewhere as an assumption

trials.justification: Strong confidence calibration with clear high/low distinction. No invented content. Async generation noted. However, Magic produced only an escalation — not a partial brief. The expected behavior requires Magic to identify what Shaq CAN implement immediately (API scaffolding, format parameter handling, date range filtering) and provide that as an actionable starting point. Instead, the escalation effectively blocks Shaq until the Option B alignment session completes. Option C acknowledges Shaq could start a spike but is not recommended, and no brief sections are provided for Shaq to work from. The escalation is well-reasoned but functionally a full block, not a partial one.

observations.text: Excellent confidence calibration — high_confidence_areas and low_confidence_areas are clearly separated with specific reasoning for each
Zero invented content — explicitly refuses to fill Bird's gaps with assumptions
Pippen's async generation concern acknowledged in high_confidence_areas as 'valid and implementation-relevant'
Escalation options are thoughtful, especially Option B which leverages MJ's existing proposals for faster resolution
Correctly identifies all three gaps (usage definition, formats, acceptance criteria) without overstating or understating
No partial brief produced — the expected behavior requires a brief with implementable sections, not just an escalation document

Shaq has no actionable starting point — the recommended path (Option B) blocks all work until the alignment session. A partial brief should have given Shaq the API endpoint structure, format parameter handling, and date range filtering to begin scaffolding
Escalation is routed to Coach K as a blocker rather than framed as a partial blocker that allows parallel work on high-confidence sections

No invented metrics — 'usage' is repeatedly flagged as undefined with multiple possible interpretations listed

Bird's vague language is NOT forwarded unchanged — each vague term is called out in terminology_alignment with explicit UNRESOLVED tags

Open questions are well-structured with owner assignment and impact assessment
